game_name: Populous II: Trials of the Olympian Gods

danger_signals.visual_form: Legendary creatures (Colossus, Medusa) wandering the map with distinctive animated sprites
danger_signals.meaning: Map control threshold reached (~75%); imminent game-ending threat; player must accelerate victory or face indestructible forces

danger_signals.visual_form: Visual path destruction left in creature's wake across terrain
danger_signals.meaning: Indiscriminate area denial; threat to both player and enemy; signals urgency to complete objective

danger_signals.visual_form: Shape-shifting fungal growth on terrain following Conway's Game of Life rules
danger_signals.meaning: Environmental hazard; lethal to any followers who step on it; visual indicator of divine intervention use

danger_signals.visual_form: Opposing deity avatar and their followers occupying territory
danger_signals.meaning: Active opposition presence; marks enemy-controlled zones and threat source

safety_signals.visual_form: Land populated by player's followers with visual distinction from enemy lands
safety_signals.meaning: Secured map space; manna generation source; zones of relative safety

safety_signals.visual_form: Temples built by followers (can appear even in enemy territory strategically)
safety_signals.meaning: Control marker; spiritual/infrastructure presence; rally point for followers; enables recovery from losing position

safety_signals.visual_form: Legendary Greek mythological figures (one per power category) rendered as distinctive units
safety_signals.meaning: Enhanced divine power deployed; temporary ally performing directed actions against enemies

safety_signals.visual_form: UI display showing manna accumulation level
safety_signals.meaning: Power availability indicator; shows resource sufficiency for divine intervention

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Zeus connection; Greek mythological aesthetic; player-selected deity association
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Followers use distinct visual palette from enemy faction
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Temples built in player territory; controlled landscape features; accumulated population density

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Each opposing god from Greek pantheon (Aphrodite, Dionysus, etc.) has thematic visual association
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Enemy followers rendered in visually distinct style; enemy territory clearly differentiated from player lands
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Enemy-controlled settlements; opposing temples; aggregated enemy population zones

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Ancient monsters (Colossus, Medusa) as faction-independent threats
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Distinct legendary creature rendering; indifferent to player/enemy allegiance
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Monster spawn zones; destructive paths; fungal hazard areas

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Map shows roughly equal player and enemy territory; both factions have visible settlements and populations
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Followers actively expand territory; conversion of neutral lands; skirmishes at faction boundaries

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Extensive player-controlled territory visible; minimal enemy presence; may trigger monster spawn visuals
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Ancient creatures appear and begin destructive wandering; player must finalize victory quickly

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Enemy territory dominates map visual; player settlements reduced; temples appear in unexpected locations as recovery mechanic
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Followers generate temples even in enemy territory for strategic recovery; power usage becomes tactical desperation

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Manifests according to power category (earth upheaval, water flooding, wind displacement, fire devastation, plant growth, or people-based effects)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Manna meter depletes visually; map terrain changes; follower behavior disrupted or enhanced depending on power type

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Manna meter UI shows critical depletion; power selection UI may gray out unavailable options
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player must rely on follower population growth for manna regeneration; strategic power selection critical

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Visual fade or transition effect; experience point reward display; advance to next opponent god
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Experience points awarded based on victory scale, power efficiency, and speed; multiplier affects subsequent map difficulty

environmental_storytelling.technique: Greek mythological theming of opponent deities
environmental_storytelling.example: Each sequential opponent represents a Greek god (Aphrodite, Dionysus, etc.) with thematic challenge design; culminates in facing Zeus himself for entry to Pantheon
Player and enemy lands rendered in visually distinct palettes; settlement density and terrain modifications indicate faction presence and power
Appearance of legendary creatures (Colossus, Medusa) signals critical map control threshold; creatures' indiscriminate destruction communicates urgency and danger elevation
Followers' ability to build temples even in enemy territory provides visual evidence of faith persistence; shows player faction's resilience through spiritual infrastructure

environmental_storytelling.technique: Manna meter as divine favor indicator
environmental_storytelling.example: Visual UI resource meter communicates player's standing with divine forces; depletion indicates power exhaustion; accumulation shows population faithfulness and growth
Six power categories (earth, water, wind, fire, plants, people) each visually transform terrain; fungal growth following Conway's Game of Life demonstrates emergent divine complexity

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Spread across map, convert neutral territory, engage enemy followers at boundaries, generate manna through population density
Build temples even in enemy territory; cluster around remaining safe zones; visual density increases in refuges
Mirror player expansion behavior; visible aggression at territory boundaries; concentrated in controlled settlement zones
Wanders map attacking enemies or performing designated nefarious acts; legendary figure with enhanced combat presence; category-specific visual design
Wanders indiscriminately between player/enemy territory; leaves trail of destruction; indestructible; kills followers regardless of faction allegiance
Shape shifts following Conway's Game of Life rules; deadly to any follower stepping on it; remains as persistent environmental danger
Larger populations generate manna faster; visual indication through follower density in controlled zones; manna availability enables divine power usage

notes: Populous II uses Greek mythological framing to communicate faction identity and progression narrative. World-state manifests primarily through territory control visualization and monster spawn mechanics. Danger signals escalate through map control thresholds, triggering indestructible creature spawns. The temple-building recovery mechanic provides visual evidence of player resilience even in losing positions. Power categories (earth/water/wind/fire/plants/people) create distinct visual communication for divine intervention types. The system lacks explicit color-faction coding but uses sprite differentiation and territory control visualization. Monster appearance at ~75% threshold serves as both danger signal and time pressure communication.
